title: Restriction enzyme digest (single + double)
description: Single and double restriction digests in 50 uL, with NEB unit and glycerol rules and Double Digest Finder buffer selection.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Cut DNA at defined recognition sites with one or two restriction enzymes, for cloning, screening, or mapping.

## Materials

| Item | Amount (50 uL reaction) |
| --- | --- |
| DNA substrate (plasmid, genomic, or PCR product) | 1 ug |
| 10X NEBuffer (rCutSmart or enzyme-specific) | 5 uL |
| Restriction enzyme | 1 uL (10 units) |
| Nuclease-free water | to 50 uL |

## Single digest (50 uL)

| Component | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| DNA | 1 ug |
| 10X NEBuffer | 5 uL |
| Enzyme | 1 uL (10 units) |
| Nuclease-free water | to 50 uL |

1. On ice, combine the DNA, 5 uL 10X NEBuffer, and water to about 43 uL. Add 1 uL (10 units) of enzyme last. Mix gently and spin down.
2. Incubate at the enzyme's optimal temperature (commonly 37 C) for 1 h. Time-Saver qualified enzymes digest in 5-15 min.
3. Heat-inactivate or column-purify if needed (per the enzyme's page, typically 65 C or 80 C for 20 min).

## Double digest (two enzymes, one tube)

Both enzymes must share a compatible buffer and incubation temperature. Use the NEB Double Digest Finder to pick the buffer giving the highest combined activity.

| Component | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| DNA | 1 ug |
| 10X NEBuffer (compatible) | 5 uL |
| Enzyme 1 | about 1 uL (10 units) |
| Enzyme 2 | about 1 uL (10 units) |
| Nuclease-free water | to 50 uL |

1. Combine on ice as above with both enzymes, keeping total enzyme volume under 10% of the reaction.
2. Incubate at the shared temperature for 1 h (5-15 min if both are Time-Saver qualified).
3. If no buffer gives both enzymes over 50% activity, or they need different temperatures, run a sequential digest: enzyme 1, then heat-inactivate or purify, then add enzyme 2.

## Expected result

DNA cut to the predicted fragment sizes. Confirm on an agarose gel against an undigested control.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- Unit definition: 1 unit fully digests 1 ug of substrate in 50 uL in 60 min at 37 C.
- Keep total enzyme volume under 10% of the reaction. Enzyme is supplied in 50% glycerol, and over 5% glycerol in the reaction can cause star activity. For 50 uL, cap enzyme at 5 uL.
- Incomplete cutting: add more units, extend the time, or check for buffer and temperature mismatch.

## Safety

BSL-1. Standard molecular-biology reagents.

## Source

Adapted from the NEB restriction digest and double-digest protocols, the unit and glycerol guidance in Optimizing Restriction Endonuclease Reactions, and the NEB Double Digest Finder.
https://www.neb.com/en-us/protocols/restriction-digest-protocol
https://www.neb.com/en-us/protocols/double-digest-protocol-with-standard-restriction-enzymes
https://www.neb.com/tools-and-resources/interactive-tools/double-digest-finder
